description: Evals covering the union of skill-making (current) and skill-creator (proposed) capabilities. Used to baseline current skill and gate changes.

evals.name: new-skill-from-scratch
evals.prompt: I want to build a skill that helps me write database migration scripts. It should check for common pitfalls like missing rollback scripts, non-transactional DDL on Postgres, and adding NOT NULL columns without defaults. Walk me through creating it.
evals.expected_output: A complete SKILL.md draft with correct frontmatter (kebab-case name, description with WHAT+WHEN+negative trigger, metadata), numbered workflow instructions, and at least one example. CHANGELOG.md stub mentioned.
expectations: Proposes a kebab-case skill name (e.g. db-migration-helper, not db_migration_helper)
Drafts a description field that includes WHAT the skill does AND WHEN to use it
Description includes at least one negative trigger (Do NOT use for...)
Produces numbered, actionable step-by-step workflow instructions
Mentions or drafts a CHANGELOG.md
Does NOT use underscores in the skill name or folder name

evals.name: review-bad-skill
evals.prompt:
Please review this skill for me:

```yaml
---
name: My_SQLHelper
description: Helps with SQL queries and database stuff.
metadata:
  version: 1.0
---

# SQL Helper

This skill helps you write SQL. Use it whenever you need SQL help.

## Steps

Just ask me about SQL and I'll help.
```
evals.expected_output: A structured review identifying: underscore/capital in name, description too vague (no negative trigger, no specific trigger phrases, no WHEN), instructions not numbered or actionable, missing author/category/tags in metadata, missing CHANGELOG.md.
expectations: Flags the underscore and capital in 'My_SQLHelper' as a naming violation
Flags the description as too vague / missing negative trigger
Flags that instructions are not numbered or actionable
Flags missing or incomplete metadata fields (author, category, tags)
Flags missing CHANGELOG.md
Provides a verdict or summary judgment (not just a list of issues)

evals.name: fix-under-triggering
evals.prompt: My skill called 'recipe-helper' never loads when users ask about meal planning or weekly meal prep, even though it should handle those cases. The current description is: 'Helps users find and adapt recipes for dietary restrictions.' How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Concrete suggestions to add trigger phrases ('meal planning', 'weekly meal prep', 'what to cook this week', 'meal schedule') to the description. Explains WHY the description doesn't match those queries. May suggest making the description slightly 'pushy' per Anthropic guidance.
expectations: Identifies that 'meal planning' and 'meal prep' are missing from the current description
Suggests specific phrases to add to the description
Explains the mechanism: Claude matches descriptions to queries by keyword/concept coverage
Produces a revised description draft (not just advice)

evals.name: scaffold-file-structure
evals.prompt: Create the file and folder structure for a new skill called 'log-analyzer' that will help me triage Kubernetes pod crash logs. Show me every file I need to create and what should go in each one.
evals.expected_output: Correct kebab-case directory 'log-analyzer/', SKILL.md with frontmatter, CHANGELOG.md stub with [1.0.0] entry, optional scripts/ and references/ mentioned. No README.md at the skill level.
expectations: Uses 'log-analyzer' (kebab-case, no underscores)
Includes SKILL.md as a required file
Includes CHANGELOG.md with at least a [1.0.0] placeholder
Does NOT include a README.md inside the skill folder
SKILL.md frontmatter includes name, description, and metadata fields
If scripts/ is included, files are placed under scripts/ not at skill root

evals.name: non-interactive-eof-fix
evals.prompt:
My skill script keeps crashing in CI with 'EOFError: EOF when reading a line'. Here's the relevant code:

```python
print('Which environment do you want to deploy to?')
print('1. staging')
print('2. production')
choice = input('Enter 1 or 2: ')
if choice == '1':
    deploy('staging')
elif choice == '2':
    deploy('production')
```

How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Provides a fix using non-interactive detection (checking sys.stdin.isatty() or CI env vars), a safe_input wrapper with EOFError handling, and an auto-fallback default for CI environments.
expectations: Provides working Python code (not just description of a fix)
Uses sys.stdin.isatty() or equivalent CI env var check (CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, etc.)
Wraps input() in a try/except that catches EOFError
Provides an automatic default/fallback when running non-interactively
The fixed code still works interactively (not just hardcoded non-interactive)

evals.name: review-progressive-disclosure-violation
evals.prompt:
Please review this skill for me:

---
name: deployment-manager
description: Manages deployments to staging and production. Use when deploying a service, rolling back a release, or checking deployment status. Do NOT use for local development.
metadata:
  author: Platform Team
  version: 1.0.0
  category: ops
  tags: [deployment, ops]
---

# Deployment Manager

Manages service deployments across environments.

## When to Use
- Deploying a new release
- Rolling back a failed deployment

**Do not use for**: local development or testing.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Select environment
Choose staging or production.

### Step 2: Run deployment
Execute the deployment script.

## Environment Reference

| Environment | URL | Region | Auth Method | Timeout | Max Retries | Health Check Path | Rollback Strategy |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| staging-us-east | https://staging-us.example.com | us-east-1 | OIDC | 300s | 3 | /health | blue-green |
| staging-eu-west | https://staging-eu.example.com | eu-west-1 | OIDC | 300s | 3 | /health | blue-green |
| prod-us-east | https://prod-us.example.com | us-east-1 | mTLS | 600s | 5 | /healthz | canary |
| prod-eu-west | https://prod-eu.example.com | eu-west-1 | mTLS | 600s | 5 | /healthz | canary |
| prod-ap-south | https://prod-ap.example.com | ap-south-1 | mTLS | 600s | 5 | /healthz | canary |
| prod-us-west | https://prod-us-west.example.com | us-west-2 | mTLS | 600s | 5 | /healthz | canary |
| prod-sa-east | https://prod-sa.example.com | sa-east-1 | mTLS | 600s | 5 | /healthz | canary |

## Error Codes

| Code | Meaning | Recovery Action | Escalation Path | SLA |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DEPLOY_001 | Health check failed | Automatic rollback | Page on-call | 5 min |
| DEPLOY_002 | Timeout exceeded | Retry with backoff | Create P1 ticket | 15 min |
| DEPLOY_003 | Auth failure | Rotate credentials | Security team | 30 min |
| DEPLOY_004 | Quota exceeded | Scale up | Infra team | 1 hour |
| DEPLOY_005 | Dependency missing | Check manifests | Owner team | 2 hours |
| DEPLOY_006 | Config invalid | Validate schema | Platform team | 1 hour |
| DEPLOY_007 | Network partition | Wait and retry | NOC | 15 min |
| DEPLOY_008 | Certificate expired | Renew cert | Security team | 30 min |
| DEPLOY_009 | Database migration failed | Rollback migration | DBA team | 30 min |
| DEPLOY_010 | Feature flag conflict | Disable flag | Product team | 15 min |
evals.expected_output: Identifies that the two tables (7 environments, 10 error codes) should be moved to references/ because they exceed 20 rows combined and are lookup data. The SKILL.md body should reference them by name with guidance on when to load.
expectations: Identifies the Environment Reference table as a progressive disclosure violation (should be in references/)
Identifies the Error Codes table as a progressive disclosure violation (should be in references/)
Explains the rule: tables longer than ~20 rows or lookup data belong in references/, not inline
Suggests specific filenames for the reference files (e.g. references/environments.md, references/error-codes.md)
Shows how the SKILL.md body should reference those files (e.g. 'Load references/environments.md for environment URLs and auth methods')
Does NOT flag the frontmatter, naming, instructions, or CHANGELOG — those are all correct in this skill
